dict.string: Used to open Terminal so you can run `claude /login` when adding an account.
Copyright © 2026 Thanh Ngô. Licensed under MIT.
Claude Bar 10.29 — menu-bar manager for multiple Claude Code accounts

Footer is gone — every global action (Add account, Verify all, Force refresh, Health check, Theme, Quit, Settings) lives in the header now. Settings anchors the top-right corner; Verify all is icon-only. MCP tab drops the per-account override rows — only the flat shared connectors list shows. No more six duplicate "using shared" entries below the canonical row. Web-linked accounts now use their WebView usage as the source of truth. OAuth (the terminal-side /api/oauth/usage path) only fires when the web scrape returned absolutely nothing for that cycle. Auto-swap threshold bar is taller, easier to grab, and stays a single accent blue instead of shifting through yellow/orange as you drag.

Fixed claude-watch refusing to let you /exit when you had manually swapped accounts mid-session. The watcher now snapshots credentials at the moment claude exits and only auto-restarts when a swap completes in the post-exit window (i.e. the actual auto-swap signature).
If your shell still has an old claude-watch process loaded (you were stuck before updating), close that terminal tab and open a new one — only new shells pick up the patched script.
